full_text:
Robert D. Schreiber & Philip D. Greenberg

Editors-in-Chief,

Cancer Immunology Research

Dear Editorial Board members,

I am pleased to share our Manuscript titled “Ex Vivo Immuno-Oncology Platform Reveals Spatial T Cell Infiltration Patterns Linked to ATR Inhibition Responses in High-Grade Serous Ovarian Cancer”. We believe that the scope of our study is a close match to that of Cancer Immunology Research.

We had the opportunity to discuss our work with Dr Karen Honey, Executive Editor, who kindly supported the submission of our manuscript to CIR.

Ovarian cancer (OC) is the deadliest gynecological malignancy contributing globally to over 200 000 deaths annually. So far, immunotherapeutic approaches have displayed disappointing results in unselected OC cohorts. Ex vivo models that recapitulate the tumor microenvironment (TME) are increasingly recognized as one of the crucial unmet needs in oncology research.

In this study, we have developed a unique functional platform established on a new physiologically relevant patient-derived matrix. Our results show that our immunocompetent patient-derived cultures (iPDCs) histologically and genomically recapitulate the microenvironment of their source tumors. Importantly, iPDC drug responses significantly correlate with the patients’ clinical responses and resistance to PARP inhibitors. Using high-throughput drug response profiling, we identify ataxia telangiectasia and Rad3-related inhibitor (ATRi) combined with a novel immunotherapy targeting Autotaxin (ATXi) as a promising new combination treatment for HGSC. For biomarker discovery, we developed a spatial analysis package (SPACEstat), which uncovered significantly increased intra- and peritumoral T-cell infiltration in tumors responding to ATRi. Interestingly we observed an augmented GzmB expression in intratumoral PD1+ CD8+T cells, linked to spatial interactions with pRPA+ tumor cells. These findings demonstrate the presence of a pre-existing intra and peritumoral CD8+T cell - spatial activation phenotype, which primes the tumors for response and serves as a biomarker for ATRi in HGSC.

The novelty and impact of our work are enlisted below:

Unique iPDC platform in a humanized matrix

Mechanisms of response and resistance using single-cell and functional analysis of tumors after immuno-oncology and combination agent treatments

Effective immunotherapy combinations in HGSC using high-throughput testing

A novel SPACEstat spatial analysis package that uncovers distinct tumor-immune spatial and functional crosstalk underpinning responses to ATRi.

We confirm that the data in this manuscript is not currently submitted to, nor published in any other primary scientific journal, and that all authors are aware of, and agree with, the contents of the manuscript.

We greatly appreciate your time in considering our submission.

Sincerely,

Anniina Färkkilä, MD PhD, Assistant Professor in Translational Gynecologic Oncology

Specialist in Obstetrics and Gynecology

Research Program in Systems Oncology &
Department of Obstetrics and Gynecology
University of Helsinki and Helsinki University Hospital
00014 University of Helsinki
anniina.farkkila@helsinki.fi
+358505967786
